blocks.body: Past Simple: Negatives — didn't + V1 for negatives in the past.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Past Simple negatives, we use did not or didn't, and the main verb goes back to V1.","rules":[{"label":"did not / didn't","color":"emerald","text":"The full form and the short form mean the same thing.","example":"I did not call. / I didn't call."},{"label":"Back to V1","color":"blue","text":"After did, the main verb returns to the base form.","example":"She didn't go. / We didn't see it."},{"label":"Same for all subjects","color":"amber","text":"The form didn't is the same with every subject.","example":"I didn't work. / He didn't work. / They didn't work."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic patterns","intro":"After did, always use V1.","items":[{"label":"I / you / we / they","title":"didn't + V1","subtitle":"I didn't call. / They didn't stay."},{"label":"he / she / it","title":"didn't + V1","subtitle":"He didn't go. / She didn't open it."},{"label":"Full form","title":"did not + V1","subtitle":"We did not watch the film."},{"label":"Contrast","title":"worked → didn't work","subtitle":"In a negative sentence, the past form disappears."}]}

{"title":"2. Why you need negatives","sections":[{"label":"The action did not happen","color":"emerald","description":"Show that an action did not happen in the past.","examples":[{"en":"I didn't see Anna yesterday.","ua":"The action did not happen."},{"en":"They didn't finish the task.","ua":"A negative past event."}]},{"label":"Contrast","color":"blue","description":"Compare what happened and what did not happen.","examples":[{"en":"She cooked dinner, but she didn't make dessert.","ua":"One action happened, another one did not."},{"en":"We visited Rome, but we didn't go to Naples.","ua":"A clear contrast in the past."}]},{"label":"One form for all","color":"amber","description":"You do not need to change did depending on the subject.","examples":[{"en":"He didn't work yesterday.","ua":"The same form as with I or they."},{"en":"They didn't work yesterday.","ua":"didn't does not change."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Affirmative and negative","intro":"Compare the positive and negative patterns.","rows":[{"en":"I worked yesterday.","ua":"I didn't work yesterday.","note":"worked → didn't work"},{"en":"She went home early.","ua":"She didn't go home early.","note":"went → didn't go"},{"en":"We saw him after class.","ua":"We didn't see him after class.","note":"saw → didn't see"}],"warning":"After didn't, do not use went, saw, or an -ed form."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using V2 after didn't.","wrong":"❌ He didn't went home.","right":"✅ He didn't go home."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Keeping -ed after didn't.","wrong":"❌ We didn't played well.","right":"✅ We didn't play well."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Making a negative sentence without did.","wrong":"❌ She not go to school yesterday.","right":"✅ She didn't go to school yesterday."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["A Past Simple negative = didn't + V1.","did not and didn't mean the same thing.","After did / didn't, the main verb always stays in V1.","The form didn't is the same for every subject."]}
